The '''''Predator''-class fighter''', also called the '''TIE Predator'''<ref name="Legacy37">[[Legacy (2006) 37|''Legacy'' (2006) 37]]</ref> and nicknamed '''Eyeballs''' by the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], was a [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] produced by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] and used by the [[Fel Empire]] as of [[130 ABY]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Predators_IK_shuttle.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Two TIE Predators attack an [[Sigma-class long-range shuttle|Imperial Knight shuttle]].]]

Considered a successor to the older [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] more than a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] prior, the TIE Predator was still highly maneuverable and heavily armed, but unlike most previous [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]], it was also equipped with [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] and a [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] generated through its blade-shaped wings. As with the earlier [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Nssis-class Clawcraft|Clawcraft]], these wings could adjust to a variety of positions, aiding the starfighter's maneuverability. They folded back fully for docking mode. However, the complex technologies packed inside the wings of the Predator were a maintenance nightmare. In addition, the standard redesigned viewport offered poor visibility due to its narrow horizontal panes separated by thick struts.<ref name="EGoW" />

After much lobbying, the Imperial Navy convinced Sienar to produce ''Predator''-class fighters with classic TIE-style cockpits that were flown by [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] leaders.<ref name="EGoW" /> Other pilots flew fighters with horizontal slats across the front of the central fuselage.

==History==
The ''Predator''-class fighters served as the primary fighter craft for the [[Fel Empire]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. After the war's end they continued to stay in use throughout the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] in service to both the [[Empire-in-exile]] and [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Krayt's Sith Empire]] until the [[Sith-Imperial starfighter]] came into service in the latter to supplant them.

In [[130 ABY]], ''Predator''-class fighters were used by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rulf Yage]]'s [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|forces]] in the [[Massacre at Ossus|attack]] on the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. When a Jedi transport attempted to flee, they moved to shoot it down but were intercepted by [[Cade Skywalker]]'s in a [[X-83 TwinTail starfighter]], who managed to defeat several TIE Predators before being shot down, buying time for the Jedi transport to escape.

[[Imperial Knight]]s would commonly fly ''Predator''-class fighters. In [[137 ABY]], [[Antares Draco]] and [[Ganner Krieg]] would take two of those fighters to [[Skirmish on Vendaxa|Vendaxa]] to save [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]] from the Sith. The [[Outer Rim Third Fleet]] used TIE Predators at the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Second Imperial Civil War)|Battle of Dac]].

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:PredatorLauchRacks.jpg|thumb|A ''Predator''-class [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] launch rack.]]

The ''Predator''-class fighter was designed by [[Sean Cooke]], a designer/illustrator handpicked by [[Dark Horse Comics]] editor [[Randy Stradley]] for the job of designing [[Legacy era|Legacy-era]] Imperial spacecraft.

When the first images of the ''Predator'' appeared, some fans speculated that the fighters with a "slatted" central pod were pilotless [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]], but this idea was ruled out by subsequent comments from the creators: in an [http://undermine.net/tracy/mirth/eu/062506legacy1.htm online chat], [[Jan Duursema]] explained that the traditional cockpits were reserved for lead fighters, and in an interview for [[Star Wars Insider 89|''Star Wars Insider'' 89]], Cooke discussed the redesign: "''I wanted to make sure it didn't look so much like the classic TIE fighter, so I redesigned the front cockpit screen, taking that kind of octagonal design and putting more vertical lines through that to simulate jaws or even prison bar cells, giving it this angry look.''"
